question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
Here's a complete example of a `charmcraft.yaml` for a Kubernetes charm, with all fields annotated:

```yaml
name: my-app-k8s          # Lowercase, hyphens only — must be unique on Charmhub
type: charm
title: My App on Kubernetes
summary: A one-line description shown in search results (< 100 chars)

description: |
  Full description of the charm, supports **markdown**.

  Appears on the Charmhub page.

# Required: OS base for building and running
bases:
  - build-on:
      - name: ubuntu
        channel: "22.04"
    run-on:
      - name: ubuntu
        channel: "22.04"

# Required: how to build the charm
parts:
  charm:
    plugin: uv
    source: .
    build-snaps:
      - astral-uv

# Required for K8s: declare you need a K8s cluster and Juju >= 3.1
assumes:
  - juju >= 3.1
  - k8s-api

# Required for K8s: sidecar containers in the pod
containers:
  my-app:
    resource: my-app-image
    mounts:
      - storage: data
        location: /var/lib/my-app

# OCI image resource backing the container above
resources:
  my-app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: "Application container image"

# Config options set via `juju config`
config:
  options:
    port:
      type: int
      description: "Port the application listens on"
      default: 8080
    log-level:
      type: string
      description: "Logging verbosity (debug, info, warning, error)"
      default: "info"
    enable-tls:
      type: boolean
      description: "Enable TLS"
      default: false

# Actions run via `juju run`
actions:
  rotate-secret:
    description: "Rotate the application secret key"
    additionalProperties: false
  flush-cache:
    description: "Flush the application cache"
    params:
      namespace:
        type: string
        description: "Cache namespace to flush (omit for all)"
    additionalProperties: false

# Relations — always include `optional` explicitly
provides:
  app-api:
    interface: http

  metrics-endpoint:
    interface: prometheus_scrape

requires:
  database:
    interface: postgresql_client
    optional: true

  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    optional: true
    limit: 1

  certificates:
    interface: tls-certificates
    optional: true

peers:
  app-peers:
    interface: my-app-peers

# Persistent storage mounted into the container
storage:
  data:
    type: filesystem
    description: "Application data directory"
    location: /var/lib/my-app
    minimum-size: 1G

# Charm library dependencies (fetched with `charmcraft fetch-libs`)
charm-libs:
  - lib: data_platform_libs.data_interfaces
    version: "0"
  - lib: traefik_k8s.v2.ingress
    version: "2"

# Links shown on Charmhub
links:
  documentation: https://discourse.charmhub.io/t/my-app-docs/12345
  issues: https://github.com/canonical/my-app-k8s/issues
  source: https://github.com/canonical/my-app-k8s
  website: https://my-app.example.com
```

## Key K8s-specific points

| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| `assumes: k8s-api` | Prevents accidental deployment on a machine model |
| `containers` | Each entry becomes a sidecar container; requires a matching `resources` entry of type `oci-image` |
| `resources` (oci-image) | The OCI image is supplied at deploy time: `juju deploy ./my-app.charm --resource my-app-image=...` |
| `storage` + `mounts` | Persistent volumes are declared in `storage` and mounted into a container via `containers[].mounts` |

## Required vs optional fields

**Required:** `name`, `type`, `bases`, `parts`, `containers` (K8s), matching `resources` for each container

**Strongly recommended:** `title`, `summary`, `description`, `assumes`, `links`

**Optional:** `config`, `actions`, `provides`, `requires`, `peers`, `storage`, `charm-libs`
